consumer_narrative:
To Whom It May Concern, This is my FINAL NOTICE before filing formal complaints with the CFPB, FTC, State Attorney General, and consideration of private legal action for your continued failure to correct multiple, ongoing FCRA violations regarding : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX Credit Card Account Ending XXXX This is now Round XXXX, and despite your previous investigations, the account is still being reported with severe inconsistencies, conflicting data, and unverifiable information, which legally requires full deletion. 

You have been notified twice already. 
You are now knowingly reporting false information. 

XXXX. FCRA XXXX ( b ) Failure to Maintain Maximum Possible Accuracy Your reporting continues to show multiple contradictory versions of the same account across all three bureaus : A. Contradictory Date Opened XXXX  : XX/XX/XXXX XXXX  : XX/XX/XXXX TransUnion : XX/XX/XXXX A credit account can not legally have three different opening dates. 

B. Contradictory Account Status XXXX  : Deleted / Closed / Charge-off XXXX  : Negative / Charge-off TransUnion : Negative / Charge-off If XXXX  deleted the account, but the same data is still being furnished to XXXX  and TransUnion, then : The furnisher is not providing consistent data Verification is impossible Deletion is mandatory XXXX Conflicting Last Activity Dates XXXX  : XX/XX/XXXX XXXX  : XX/XX/XXXX TransUnion : XX/XX/XXXX XXXX bureaus can not produce XXXX different dates for the same transaction. 

This makes the account inherently unverifiable. 

XXXX Inconsistent Payment History XXXX  is reporting multiple lates : XXXX, XXXX, XXXX, 150 days late, and then suddenly Back to OK, then CO This pattern is incoherent and impossible under FCRA guidelines, and demonstrates incorrect automated furnishing. 

The presence of XXXX delinquency followed by XXXX can not be supported by any real payment record. 

XXXX Different Date of Last Payment XXXX  : XX/XX/XXXX XXXX  : XX/XX/XXXX TransUnion : XX/XX/XXXX If the bureaus can not agree on the last payment date, then none of the reporting is reliable. 

XXXX. FCRA 1681i Failure to Conduct a Reasonable Reinvestigation After two rounds of disputes : None of the conflicting data has been corrected No explanation was provided for the multiple inconsistencies No Method of Verification was provided No documentary proof from the furnisher was provided Verified as accurate was returned despite clear, obvious errors This proves that your investigation was : Automated Superficial Non-compliant Not legally sufficient This is a direct violation of 1681i.

3. FCRA 1681s-2 ( a ) Furnisher ( XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX Reporting Conflicting Data A furnisher can not legally report XXXX different versions of the same account. 

XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX is providing : Different dates opened Different last activity Different last payment Different charge-off dates Different payment histories Different statuses This makes the account factually unverified and legally unreportable. 

Under federal law, unverifiable data must be deleted. 

XXXX. Your Continued Reporting Is Now Willful ( FCRA 616 ) Because you have been notified twice already, your continued reporting of inaccurate information constitutes : Willful noncompliance Negligent damage to the consumer Violation of my rights under federal law This exposes each bureau to civil liability.

product: Mortgage
issue: Applying for a mortgage or refinancing an existing mortgage
consumer_narrative: I applied originally for refinance in XXXX and was told the credit requirements. Weeks later credit requirements changed and I was postponed and recommended I pay a couple of items off. After jumping through all the hoops to get my credit to where they needed it to be in XXXX, I was told I would have a closing disclosure by XX/XX/XXXX. I cant recall what delayed the closing that time, but in XXXX I completed a XXXX withdrawal to pay off a debt at their recommendation. They recorded it incorrectly so I had to ask a family member to borrow {$2500.00} mid XXXX so it would look like the XXXX money was in my savings because they incorrectly recorded it that way instead of being used to payoff debt. I was assured this would allow me to close, this time no later than mid XXXX. They decided to switch me from conventional to FHA a few times, and I wasnt really given a good reason other than this would be a better deal for me and I was told most recently we would definitely close in XXXX. Local office staff members XXXX and XXXX admitted these mistakes in late XXXX and have apologized, assuring me they would be in a position by XXXX to request my loan be rushed to close in XXXX and could be closed by XX/XX/XXXX if approved. They offered me the end of last week around {$2000.00} as pain and suffering ( their offer, their words, not mine ) per XXXX XXXX regional manager and but that the rush was denied and it would now be XXXX before we could close. I asked to speak with XXXX XXXX for an explanation of why we can not expedite the loan closing at this point. Today, XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX XXXX was very condescending and disrespectful blaming my credit for the delays on closing and again offering almost {$2000.00} to make it right as an olive branch but he called me rude and angry, neither of which are true. After almost an hour and him apologizing for his initial tone with me, I agreed to the terms of closing the loan in XXXX and accepting the money offered to make the mistakes right. When I asked for this in writing because in the past few months so many promises had been made only to be broken and have people deny they made them to begin with, he agreed then said instead he was going to transfer my mortgage and not providing the payment offered previously. I expressed a desire to keep our arrangements to avoid the need of starting over completely once again and he refused. I asked if I could speak with his superior or CEO he refused and hung up on me. I am now forced to start the entire process over from the start after waiting almost 6 months to refinance with them. I am a widowed woman with children, working in XXXXealthcare during a worldwide pandemic and this entire process has put undue financial burden and added stress on me. If they did not wish to work with me due to my unattractive credit which was what XXXX blamed for the delays, they should have admitted that and referred me to a lender who could and would help me.

consumer_narrative:
I hold the durable power of attorney for my aunt who is medically unable to handle her finances and other items. Earlier last year we decided to move all of her banking from XXXX and XXXX XXXX to USAA Federal Savings Bank. This process was extremely difficult because even USAA didn't understand their own process for establishing a power of attorney on a new account. We were forced to deal with the same account lock out situation. After several weeks, the account was set up with the power of attorney and I was able to manage my aunt 's financial obligations. 

Today ( XX/XX/2018 ) when I tried to access her account, I received an error message indicating the account had been locked because contact information was incorrect. This seemed odd as I did not recently provide any contact information. I called the phone number that was provided to unlock the account and was told I would have to call back in several days. They refused to give me any reason for the account lock and hung up on me. 

I understand that sometimes security issues arise but that is no excuse for not notifying the member or their representatives ( as specified in the account details ) that were created when the account was opened that access may be limited during a security review. If USAA doesn't understand their own processes for POA 's on an account, perhaps they should review their policies and procedures and provide remedial training.

I was informed my accounts were closed XX/XX/20 by not only tellers & bankers at the branch but over the phone as well.

In the fine print from bank of America it states anybody that has any problems or questions regarding your account/ bank statements you have 30 days & in some cases 60 days yet bank of America went back 4 months Regardless the fact they initiated a claim past the  allotted stated time frame nobody did any kind of due diligence and compared other employees checks with the ones in question even to payroll but never happened. 
So in the last 4 months my accounts were wrongly frozen over {$10000.00} taken out & awarded to the disgruntled employer nobody actually doing their job & investigating Then my account was i guess oxidant closed as of XX/XX/20 & was told no matter how many claims I open they weren't going to reopen/ relinquish about it because they already made a decision ( without even looking at evidence in the first place ) I want my money back that the bank stole from me

product: Student loan
issue: Dealing with your lender or servicer
sub_issue: Trouble with how payments are being handled
consumer_narrative:
I am a recipient of the XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. Through this program, the XXXX makes quarterly payments towards my loans on my behalf. The XXXX payments are much larger than my monthly bill ( XXXX times larger ), so each quarterly payment puts me in paid ahead status for approximately XXXX to XXXX  months, which allows me to not make payments in the months between quarterly XXXX payments. I have been on this program for several years and had no issues when my loans were previously with XXXX. As of this spring, my loans were transferred to XXXX and they have misapplied these XXXX payments, made false claims, and then lost the payment. 

I received my welcome letter from XXXX on XX/XX/year> and notification of my recertification of my income driven repayment plan on XX/XX/year>. My beginning balance was {$54000.00} and my first monthly payment was due on XX/XX/year> in the amount of {$1200.00}. On XX/XX/year> the XXXX made a payment on my behalf in the amount of {$10000.00}. The payment was received by XXXX on XX/XX/year> and I provided documentation to the XXXX that the payment had reached my account on XX/XX/year>. At this time, my loan balance was {$44000.00}. The only issue was that the payment did not put me in paid-ahead status so my account stated that I had another payment due on XX/XX/year>. 

I reached out on XX/XX/year> to have the payment applied correctly so it would put me in paid ahead status. The XXXX representative told me that they did not have a record of how the payment was made but assured me that they had received it and my account had been credited. The representative put in a request to have the payment reapplied and told me Id hear back in XXXX  days. When I had not heard back and I had not been placed in paid ahead status I reached out again on XX/XX/year>. They XXXX representative stated that they could not provide a reason for why the payment hadnt been reapplied but said to reach back out in XXXX  days and that theyd have an answer. When my account still had not been updated I reached out again on XX/XX/year>. This time the representative stated that I cant be in paid ahead status when XXXX on an income driven repayment plan, which is incorrect. I had been in paid ahead status with an income driven repayment plan the entire time my loans were with XXXX. 

As Ive been trying to sort this out, I did not make an XXXX payment ( due XX/XX/year> ) because the payment was not expected, my wife and I are paying for XXXX  out of pocket, and we are unable to afford the payment at this time. Also, while this was going on, I had also reached out to the XXXX XXXX Ombudsman for support, opening a case on XX/XX/year> and providing updates throughout the process. After my contact with XXXX on XX/XX/year> and no response or assistance from the XXXX XXXX Ombudsman, I reached out to my congressional representative to have them advocate on my behalf. Soon after ( XX/XX/year> ), I received my first response about the case I had opened with the Ombudsman. The email came from XXXX customer service department rather than the Ombudsmans office. The email stated that my paid-ahead status was revoked when my income driven repayment plan was recertified. However, this is also a mistake or incorrect since my most recent payment was received on XX/XX/year>, after my recertification on XX/XX/year>. 

Today ( XX/XX/year> ), I received a call from XXXX stating that the payment made by the XXXX on XX/XX/year> had been reversed and that my account is now overdue in the amount of the original payment. However, my account summary on the XXXX website shows the balance after the payment was made on XX/XX/year> ( {$44000.00} ) and it shows my amount due as {$2400.00}, which would be correct since I didnt make the XXXX payment and have a XXXX payment due. The representative refused to believe that my online account displayed different information than her own and was adamant that I had reversed the payment. Additionally, every other contact Ive had with XXXX, they have recognized that the payment had been made. When I asked them if there is anyone else I can talk to explain my case, they put me on hold for XXXX minutes and then stated that no one was available. I did not request that the payment be reversed by the XXXX and they did not initiate the process. 

I am confident that XXXX lost track of my payment when they were trying to reapply it to my loan and are now claiming that I am at fault. I have documentation to support everything Ive stated.

product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports
consumer_narrative:
The Fair Credit Reporting Act ( 15 U.S. Code 1681 ) says ( 1 ) The banking system is dependent upon fair and accurate credit reporting. Inaccurate credit reports directly impair the efficiency of the banking system, and unfair credit reporting methods undermine the public confidence which is essential to the continued functioning of the banking system.

So whenever there is a violation under the FCRA, it impairs the efficiency of the banking system. This is a violation of my privacy with my consumer report, since I control what is listed and what is not. In the event that any accounts are reported without my written consent, that is considered identity theft.

This is a violation of both 15 U.S. Code 1681b ( 2 ) and 15 U.S. Code 1681c-2. This leads way to civil liability against the credit reporting agency for negligent and willful noncompliance under 15 U.S. Code 1681n, which allows for {$1000.00} per violation, for account listed without my expressed written consent.

Complaint Regarding Inaccessible Experian Account and Lack of Support I am submitting this complaint because I have been unable to access my Experian account, which has prevented me from viewing my credit score and obtaining my annual credit report. This has been an ongoing issue, and I am extremely frustrated with the lack of accessible customer support from Experian.

The account is currently locked, and when I try to recover it, Im required to verify using my old email address and old phone number, both of which I no longer have access to. This has made it impossible for me to get back into the account. When I attempt to call Experian, the automated system does not allow me to speak with a live representative unless I can verify the old contact informationwhich I can not. 

This issue is further complicated by the fact that my computer and XXXX were hacked last year, which compromised several of my accounts, including my email. Since then, Ive been locked out of multiple platforms due to compromised credentials, including my Experian account. I have no reliable way to check my credit or monitor for further fraud or identity theft, and this has created a serious barrier to managing my financial health. 

I am reaching out to the CFPB in hopes that you can help me connect with someone at Experian who can assist me with verifying my identity through alternate means, so that I can regain access to my account. I am also open to any guidance or advice from others who may have experienced similar issues and successfully resolved them. 

Thank you for your attention to this matter. I am sincerely hoping for a resolution soon.

I tried to access my free credit report from TransUnion on line but TransUnion did not accept the information I provided. I was able to order a report by phone using my social security number, but the report I received was in the name of a woman with the same first name as me and a social security number that matched 8 of the 9 social security digits. The address, phone numbers and birth date matched mine but the credit accounts included both hers and mine. I finally got the fraud department to check into separating the two files after my letter requesting it was ignored with the complaint they could n't read my driver 's license, which I believe is purposefully designed to not be copied and scanned. I requested a credit freeze but was told I could n't because of the commingled file. TransUnion put a security freeze on my file with my cell phone number for verification, but the freeze ended up on the other woman 's account with my phone number. I am still getting confirmations from XXXX in the other woman 's name at my address. I have received someone else 's credit report and social security number in the mail. Her credit report also listed her address and phone number in the file. This is third time this woman 's information has ended up in my file and it is worse this time because I am retired and cutting back on my debt while she is increasing hers. TransUnion uses social security numbers as identifiers but seems more interested in like numbers than exact numbers. TransUnion will not give me a free freeze and I do not know how to protect myself from losing my credit identity again.

consumer_narrative:
On XX/XX/2021, I mailed a Principal Only payment in the amount of {$450.00} to Toyota Financial Services that was to be applied to my vehicle loan with them. Payment was in the form of my personal check # XXXX. The check, and the payment coupon that was mailed with the check, was clearly marked Principal Only. The copy of my check which I was able to get from my bank was marked on the front as received on XX/XX/XXXX and has the initials XXXX. Additionally, it was stamped on the back with a Toyota stamp that says For Deposit Only on XX/XX/XXXX. 

On XX/XX/2021, after not having received credit for the payment, I made my first call to Toyota in an attempt to receive credit for my payment. Following is a detailed accounting of the many attempts I have made to receive credit : XXXX. XX/XX/2021 XXXX Thursday XXXX I called Toyota Financial Services ( TFS ) and spoke with XXXX XXXX I.D . # XXXX XXXX. XXXX said it might take more time since a lot of employees are working from home due to the pandemic. If it had not posted by the end of the day or by tomorrow ( Friday XXXX, I should call back on Monday. Also on Monday, a copy of the front and back of the negotiated check should be uploaded onto the TFS website. Give it a day or two after uploading the copy. 
XXXX. XX/XX/2021 XXXX Monday ) A copy of my XXXX  XXXX XXXX check transaction was uploaded onto the website and I received a confirmation from the website that the copy of the check was received. 
XXXX. XX/XX/2021 ( Friday XXXX I called TFS since I still had not received credit for the check. Spoke with XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX  . XXXX XXXX and XXXX XXXX I.D . XXXX ). I was told they could not locate the copy of the check that was uploaded onto the website and I should fax the copy. Since I do not have a fax machine, I had to drive to XXXX and pay to have the copy faxed. As instructed by XXXX, I faxed the copy to the attention of XXXX at fax no. XXXX. 
XXXX. XX/XX/2021 XXXX Friday ) I called TFS later in the day to confirm that they had received my fax. I was told it takes 24 to 48 hours for the information to get posted into the system so other agents could see it. 
XXXX. XX/XX/2021 XXXX Thursday XXXX I called TFS and spoke with XXXX XXXX I.D . XXXX XXXX XXXX  XXXX said they received the fax on XX/XX/XXXX and said he would put me on hold while he checked to see why it is taking so long to have my account credited. After coming back on the telephone, he said the money would be refunded to me. I asked that instead of refunding the money, that it be applied as a Principal Only payment. He said he would make a note of this. He will submit all information after which it would be reviewed. He would see to it that this is processed. Should be completed by Monday, XX/XX/2021. 
XXXX. XX/XX/2021 XXXX Wednesday XXXX I called TFS and asked XXXX, who answered the telephone, if I could speak with a manager. I was connected with XXXX who identified herself as a Resolution Specialist. By the way, each time I call TFS, I had to give a full explanation of all that I had gone through thus far. After giving XXXX my full explanation, she put me on hold while she researched my file. She said she was able to find the copy of the negotiated check that had been uploaded onto the website on XX/XX/XXXX as well as a copy of the TFS confirmation. She additionally said she would have my account credited. It would take three to five business days to have it credited. 
XXXX. XX/XX/2021 XXXX Monday XXXX I called XXXX and spoke with XXXX XXXX I.D . # XXXX XXXX XXXX stated that XXXX had not created the ticket that was required to have my account credited. He stated he would create the ticket and would have the payment credited towards my principal. The account should be credited by Thursday, XX/XX/2021. 

As of today, XX/XX/2021, my account has still not been credited with my {$450.00} payment. Since it has been verified XXXX via the copy of my check that was received from XXXX  XXXX XXXX XXXX that they were in receipt of my check on XX/XX/2021, my account should also be credited with the interest charged on the {$450.00} through the date that I receive credit for the payment.

consumer_narrative:
No Parking Rule/Refusal to pay/ Cease and Desist I Refuse to Pay this alleged collection XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. 
XXXX, XXXX  XXXX XX/XX/XXXX NATIONAL CREDIT ADJUSTERS  , L.L.C XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, KS XXXX To Whom It XXXX Concern : This notice is to inform you that you have unlawfully reported an alleged debt to my consumer report. Under the New Rule REGULATION F which took effect XX/XX/XXXX, You NATIONAL CREDIT ADJUSTERS , L.L.C You have violated the FDCPA 1006.30a and its new rule by parking this alleged debt on my consumer reports. Cease and Desist the reporting of this alleged XXXX immediately or I will escalate this matter to taking legal action. 
I have not given you written permission to put anything on my credit profile 15 USC 1681b2.

You have no permissible purpose by law to contact third parties with my private or personal information. Your offenses amount to Aggravated Identity Theft pursuant to 18 U.S.C. 1028A. You have knowingly transferred, possessed, or used, without lawful authority, a means of identification of me, which is a felony punishable with up to 2 years of imprisonment, in addition to civil liability.

I AM A LITIGIOUS CONSUMER I WILL NOT HESITATE TO TAKE LEGAL ACTION AGAINST NATIONAL CREDIT ADJUSTERS , L.L.C 15 USC 1692c ( c ) Ceasing communication If a consumer notifies a debt collector in writing that the consumer refuses to pay a debt or that the consumer wishes the debt collector to cease further communication with the consumer, the debt collector shall not communicate further with the consumer with respect to such debt. 
I refuse to pay this alleged debt. Cease and Desist all collections activity regarding this alleged debt and PROMPTLY Delete it from all consumer reporting agencies that you have reported this inaccurate, incomplete, erroneous and misleading information to at once.

2.If the above documentation can not be produced, the continued reporting of these accounts is inaccurate and must be deleted.

3.Under the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act ( GLBA ), furnishers of credit information are obligated to safeguard consumer data. Reporting unverifiable information while distributing my personal details constitutes a violation.

Requested Relief : I request a full investigation into the accounts listed above.

If the promissory notes, chain of custody, and complete payment history can not be provided, I demand the immediate deletion of these accounts from my credit reports with XXXX, Experian, and XXXX.

issue: Problem caused by your funds being low
consumer_narrative:
On XX/XX/year>, my Bank of America online profile was compromised. An unauthorized payee was added to my account, and several wire transfers were subsequently initiated to this same payee. I received alerts indicating that my user ID had been accessed, my password had been changed, and a new beneficiary had been added. I immediately contacted Bank of America to report each of these suspicious activities. 

I was initially informed that the wire transfers had been flagged and placed on hold ; however, my available balance was still affected. After multiple unsuccessful attempts to resolve the issue remotely, I flew from XXXX to XXXX on XX/XX/year>, and visited the Bank of America branch in XXXX XXXX, where I met with XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX ). Bank of America conducted an internal investigation and confirmed the fraud. On XX/XX/XXXX, a reimbursement of {$54000.00} USD was deposited into my account, covering the fraudulent transactions and related fees. 

During this branch visit, I requested the closure of my checking account ending in XXXX. However, the bank declined, citing the ongoing investigation. I also took all possible security measures by changing my user credentials and enabling XXXX XXXX  authentication. 

Unfortunately, despite these steps, the breach occurred again on XX/XX/XXXX. My password was reset, XXXX XXXX  was disabled, and the same fraudulent payee was re-added. Additional wire transfers were initiated, again triggering security alerts. I contacted Bank of America immediately and was told that wire transfer capabilities had been removed and all beneficiaries deleted. I verified these changes through my online banking access, but I continued to receive alerts and observed unauthorized transactions on my account. 

The recurrence of this breach, involving the same recipient and similar transaction patterns, demonstrates a systemic failure in securing my accounts and preventing repeat fraud. Despite my diligence and direct communication with the bank, my account remains vulnerable, and I have suffered repeated financial and emotional distress as a result. 

I am requesting the CFPBs assistance in investigating this matter, ensuring that Bank of America upholds its responsibilities in protecting consumer accounts, and securing a full resolutionincluding the return of all funds lost due to this breach.

sub_issue: Reporting company used your report improperly
consumer_narrative:
I am writing to dispute the late payments that have been reported on my credit report. According to my records, I have made all payments on time and in full, and I believe that these late payment entries are inaccurate. 

Under the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) and the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act ( FDCPA ), consumers have certain rights and protections when it comes to credit reporting. Specifically, I would like to draw your attention to the following provisions : 15 USC 1681i : This section outlines the procedure for disputing inaccuracies on credit reports. As a consumer, I have reviewed my credit report and identified the following late payment entries that I dispute : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX 

I request that you investigate these entries and remove them from my credit report if they are found to be inaccurate. I also request that you provide me with a written explanation of your investigation results and any corrections made to my credit report. 

15 USC 1681b : This section requires that credit reporting agencies only provide credit reports to parties that have a legitimate purpose for accessing them. I ask that you confirm that you had a legitimate purpose for reporting the late payments to the credit reporting agency in question.

15 USC 1666b : This section requires that creditors provide written notice to consumers when adverse actions are taken, such as reporting late payments to credit reporting agencies. I request that you confirm that you provided me with the required notice prior to reporting the late payments to the credit reporting agency.

Furthermore, I remind you of your obligation under the FDCPA to provide accurate information to credit reporting agencies. Failing to do so could be considered a violation of the Act and could result in legal action being taken against you.

Thank you for your attention to this matter. Please provide me with any necessary documentation or information to assist me in this dispute process. 

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX

consumer_narrative:
This is todays follow up to the following cases : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ; From your most recent messages ( attached ) it is clear the Experian lacks an understanding of the full scope of its legal obligations. It would also appear that Experian continues to initiate disputes on behalf of consumers not only without their knowledge or consent but even against the consumers demand Experian not do so. 

Experian 's obligations are not limited to Section 611 ( a ) ( 3 ) ( A ) of the Fair Credit Reporting Act. In fact Section 611 ( 2 ) ( 3 ) ( A ) has no relevance to the matter of concern, as it pertains to " Frivolous Disputes '' and the present pertains to information that has been reported to be the result of identity theft. Section 605B is the appropriate portion of the FCRA which outlines Experian 's duties, which include " ( a ) Block. Except as otherwise provided in this section, a consumer reporting agency shall block the reporting of any information in the file of a consumer that the consumer identifies as information that resulted from an alleged identity theft, not later than 4 business days ... '' This matter was first brought to Experian 's attention on XX/XX/2023, thus it had until XX/XX/2023 at the very latest to block the information and it did not and still has not. This means that each and every day from XX/XX/2023 through today and continuing until Experian fulfills its legal obligation, it has and will continue to cause me to suffer irreparable harm, including but not limited to severe injuries to my mental and emotional health, extreme and undue hardship including causing me to become homeless since you have been informing prospective landlords and creditors that I am a substantial risk, when I am not, and obstruction of my businesses ability to gain the credit needed to sustain. 

Even if Experian clams to be ignorant of all its obligations outlined in the FCRA, including those which are detailed above, it still would not absolve you of liability for the destruction you have done. When I get behind the wheel of a vehicle, my ignorance of a traffic law doesn't make me not guilty when I get ticketed and found guilty of violating said law, because I have an obligation to understand all that is legally required of me before I can drive. The same goes for Experian. From the day Experian become a credit reporting agency, it became responsible for the legal obligations of such an entity, regardless of how aware or not it might have been of them.

I found a townhome for $ XXXX, I immediately contact Ms. XXXX informing her that I want to do the second home option. Ms.XXXX  contacts Mr. XXXX requesting a pre-approval for $ XXXX. She received, made the offer, and it was accepted. On XX/XX/XXXX, I received an email from Mr. XXXX stated he needed my monthly income, I email it to him. I received another email requesting a copy of all bank statements ASAP. I send and text him it was completed and please confirm once received. He did confirm received. I received another email stated he needed a copy of my co-signer retirement. I replied informing him I told him when asked by XXXX whether or not will I use and co-signer, I told her no because the property amount was lower than the property I was purchasing in XXXX. The pre-approval that XXXX received only listed me as the applicant. I expressed to Ms.XXXX Mr. XXXX disrespectful ; insulting questions and inappropriate comments. I expressed that I know each state was different, but his behavior has nothing to do with state real estate. He does not respond to my question on email or text. He asked me about XXXX ( I do not know if that's someone's first or last name ) and my co-signer is XXXX XXXX, I asked did he mean XXXX XXXX or was he confusing me with another client. I received no responses. He emails me requesting documents that he confirmed he received. He stated he is working remotely and can not remember. I asked where my documents following the privacy act, because I had sent the same documents multiply times. I again, express my concern with Ms. XXXX, she agrees to speak with Mr. XXXX and also Ms. XXXX XXXX to ensure we are on the same accord and moving forward. Also, we concerned about working with another lender because of the dissatisfaction with the services, I am receiving. Ms.XXXX spoke with Ms. XXXX and she was going to give me a call letter that day. I received a call and express what was according to XXXX XXXX ( branch manager of Greentree Mortgage ). She informed me she would review my documents and give me a call. I informed her that I was flying into New Jersey that night and feel free to email me if anything is needed. She requests another copy of my XXXX w2, I email and asked her to confirmed received. I did not receive a call so I contact Ms. XXXX for an update. She stated that I would need a co-signer. I asked why, and she stated that they have to consider my rent from my primary residence, I informed her that I am currently in XXXX XXXX residing with family and I do not pay any rent. I informed her the only debt I have is student loans. She stated my car payment and informed her that it was payoff by the insurance company and I have a letter from XXXX and a copy of the transfer of title. It was paid off in XXXX and I was informed it would take a few weeks before it shows paid. I offered to send those documents and because she did not believe I was in XXXX XXXX proof of flight and PODs storage receipt. I asked for a corporate email to express the treatment I was receiving. She stated she would have to find it giving that Mr. XXXX is the branch manager. On XXXX XXXX Ms. XXXX stated that she was sending my file to the underwritten supervisor to review. The next morning I received, an email requesting a letter from my employer the questions are : 1. Will the letter say that she is a fully remote teacher and can work permanently from XXXX? 
2.As far as continuance of income, I assume she only has a 1-year contract. Will the school say the contract can be renewed with her working out of state? 

I do not understand if it's a second mortgage why would it provide a letter indicating permanently resides in New Jersey. How can anything be promised job security for next year? Also, I had informed Mr. XXXX that I will seek employment in the tri-state area. I provide proof of on had monies that could cover the home expensive. 

I asked for the underwritten supervisor 's contact information for letter clarification. Ms. XXXX stated : Hi XXXX, We are not permitted to release their contact information. Underwriters do not speak with borrowers/clients. When you provide the information they requested, we would email it back to them for their review. 
Again, I requested a copy of all my documents : underwritten, credit report, and the pre-approval. I have not received them.

Please reprimand this organization fir preying on weakened businesses that have barely been able to catch our breathe post mid pandemic. 

I want them to fix the late pay. I paid them their {$1100.00} including late fee added XX/XX/XXXX. Should they have been able to change the contract repayment terms mid contract and report me as a bad payor as a result of their failure to refund me the $ $ the took two times in one month?

product: Credit reporting or other personal consumer reports
issue: Problem with a company's investigation into an existing problem
sub_issue: Their investigation did not fix an error on your report
consumer_narrative:
I am filing a formal complaint regarding the actions of Equifax XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, as they have violated multiple provisions of the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) and other privacy laws concerning my personal information and consumer rights. 
First and foremost, I have been affected by Equifaxs failure to protect my personal data, particularly in light of their history of data breaches. Due to their lack of diligence, I have reasonable concerns that my personal information is at risk, making me more vulnerable to continued identity theft and fraudulent activities. Despite my repeated efforts to dispute inaccurate information on my credit report, Equifax has continued to mishandle my data and fail in its legal duty to ensure fair and accurate reporting. 
Violations Committed by Equifax 1. Failure to Ensure Accuracy of Consumer Reports ( FCRA Violation - XXXX U.S.C. XXXX ( b ) ) Equifax has continued to report incorrect information on my credit report, despite multiple dispute attempts. 
This includes unauthorized hard inquiries, incorrect addresses, and inaccurate account balances. 
As a result, my credit score has been negatively impacted, leading to credit denials and financial harm. 
2. Unauthorized Disclosure of Personal Information ( XXXX U.S.C. XXXX & XXXX U.S.C. XXXX ) Equifax furnished my consumer report without my written consent, violating FCRAs permissible purpose requirements. 
Under XXXX U.S.C. XXXX, financial institutions are required to protect the confidentiality of nonpublic personal information, which Equifax has failed to do. 
3. Violation of Opt-Out Rights ( XXXX U.S.C. XXXX & XXXX XXXX XXXX ) Equifax has shared my personal and financial data with third parties without properly informing me of my right to opt out. 
I was never given the required opt-out notice, which is a direct violation of my consumer rights. 
5. Failure to Investigate and Correct Disputed Information ( XXXX U.S.C. XXXX ( a ) ) Equifax has engaged in stalling tactics, repeatedly asking for identity verification, despite already receiving multiple forms of identification from me. 
Despite my disputes, Equifax has refused to remove incorrect data and continues to report misleading financial information that damages my credit standing. 
Financial & Emotional Damages Incurred Due to Equifaxs negligent actions, I have suffered financial losses and emotional distress, including : Denial of credit opportunities, including a home loan and business financing. 
Increased interest rates on existing accounts due to Equifaxs inaccurate reporting. 
Emotional distress, anxiety, and frustration due to the continued mishandling of my personal data.
